member.name: Bridget Phillipson
member.role: MP for Houghton and Sunderland South
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Bridget Phillipson is the Labour MP for Houghton and Sunderland South , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010. She currently holds the Government posts of Secretary of State for Education, and Minister for Women and Equalities.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, when she plans to publish the 2024 School Teachers’ Review Body report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The School Teachers’ Review Body (STRB) makes recommendations on the pay of teachers in England and reports to my right hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Education, and my right hon. Friend, the Prime Minister.As part of the normal process, the STRB...

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she plans to take to evaluate the recommendations of the 2024 School Teachers’ Review Body report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The School Teachers’ Review Body (STRB) makes recommendations on the pay of teachers in England and reports to my right hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Education, and my right hon. Friend, the Prime Minister.As part of the normal process, the STRB...

writtenQuestions.heading: Childcare and Pre-school Education: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to count the number of staff working in the early years and childcare sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department collects information on the overall size of the workforce through its regular Survey of Childcare and Early Years Providers, which can be found at: https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/childcare-and-early-years-providers-survey-2023. Th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Childcare: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the impact of her policies on the childcare workforce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In accordance with standard practice, new policies which have been introduced to support the early years workforce will be evaluated to understand their impact and effectiveness. This includes the recently announced national recruitment campaign and early...
